behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: rename detection comes from real `git` subprocess output and flags; a fake parser fixture can hide failures where the CLI invocation or git options produce add+delete instead of rename. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: an Integration test with mocked `git diff --name-status` could pass with `R100`, while the real command (e.g., missing rename detection behavior in invocation) yields `A`/`D`, causing double counting. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is purely git diff categorization with no AI/ML step.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against the repo via subprocess, and assert report shows the file only under `renamed` and not in `added`/`deleted`.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: binary diff behavior depends on real git subprocess output and encoding/bytes handling. Mocked text output often misses real binary markers/exit behavior and decode edge cases. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration with stubbed diff text can pass, while real git output for binary files triggers decode/parsing exceptions and crashes. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (no AI/ML, no multi-agent pipeline).
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, commit a binary file change (and optionally another binary add), run CLI as a subprocess, assert exit code is success and report includes binary files in counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: this failure boundary is real git repository state plus subprocess exit/error semantics in an unborn branch. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git calls can mask actual non-zero exit codes/messages from `git` in a no-commit repo. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: an Integration fake returning empty data passes, while real git returns error codes that can bubble up and fail the CLI. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior text is repository-state handling only.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with no commits, run the CLI subprocess, assert successful execution and structured empty report on stdout.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS/REAL-INFRA: correctness depends on end-to-end interaction between repo history, git subprocess output, and report aggregation. Integration with canned outputs can miss mismatches caused by real git history semantics (renames, mixed commit states, path handling). LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: mocked history snapshots may pass aggregation, but real repository history produces statuses/order/details that expose counting bugs. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text (deterministic git history accounting, no AI/ML decisions).
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history with known adds/deletes/renames/modifications across commits, run CLI subprocess, and assert category counts equal expected totals derived from the constructed history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: staged vs unstaged semantics are git index/working-tree behaviors exposed via real subprocess calls. MOCK-HIDE: faking repository status can hide divergences in command selection/flags between staged and unstaged paths. LOWER-LEVEL-MISS-PROOF: Integration stubs can return identical fixtures for both modes, while real git state differences reveal inconsistent CLI output. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because behavior is deterministic git state comparison without AI/ML or multi-agent steps.
behaviors.test_description: Create identical logical edits in two real repos (or sequentially reset in one): one staged, one unstaged; run CLI subprocess for each mode and assert stdout structured report is byte-identical.
